title: What’s going on with Developers and AI?
summary: The video discusses how AI is reshaping software development, tracing the evolution from early accessible coding to the modern era of large language models. It highlights how AI tools like Google AI Studio are empowering developers and argues that this is an exciting time for the profession.
writeup: A developer traces the arc of programming accessibility from TRS-80 BASIC in the 1980s, through the Stack Overflow era that dominated roughly 1999 to 2022, to AI-assisted coding in IDEs like Cursor and, by 2026, full app generation from voice prompts in Google AI Studio. The through-line is a tension between excitement (developers now wield power tools) and vertigo (the core skill of knowing which characters to write is being automated). The talk closes with a live voice-to-app demo that rebuilds the speaker's first childhood program, a TRS-80 BASIC loop, as a browser app generated in minutes.

key_points: The dominant programming workflow from the late 1990s to 2022 was: write code, hit a problem, Google it, land on Stack Overflow, copy-paste the answer, and tweak it for the specific case.

Cursor moved AI chat directly into the IDE, giving the model automatic access to the developer's full file system and codebase as context, producing more accurate and personalized answers than a separate browser tab.

By 2026, Google AI Studio (aistudio.google.com) can accept a voice description and generate a functioning app, complete with stylesheets and metadata, in a couple of minutes, with no installation required.
The power-tools analogy frames the unresolved question: a circular saw does not eliminate the need for skill, but it does change what volume of output is expected and who can begin building.

Programming was broadly accessible in the early 1980s because BASIC was the only interface to machines like the TRS-80, and magazines published type-it-in game listings that taught a generation by copying; that accessibility was later lost as languages and tooling grew more complex.
